edges.description: Deregulated cilium-dependent Hedgehog/GLI signaling disrupts skeletal (epiphyseal), craniofacial, and head-growth patterning.
edges.source: KIF7 Cilium-Tip Dysfunction and Impaired Hedgehog Signal Transduction

edges.description: Abnormal cilium-dependent skeletal patterning produces multiple epiphyseal dysplasia.
Abnormal craniofacial patterning produces the distinctive facial appearance.

nodes.description: A distinctive (dysmorphic) facial appearance is one of the three defining features of the syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: A mutation in KIF7 is responsible for the autosomal recessive syndrome of macrocephaly, multiple epiphyseal dysplasia and distinctive facial appearance.
evidence.snippet: macrocephaly, multiple epiphyseal dysplasia and distinctive facial appearance

nodes.description: KIF7, the human ortholog of Drosophila Costal2, is an immotile kinesin-4 protein that localizes to the tip of the primary cilium, where it regulates the GLI transcription factors of the Hedgehog pathway. In the absence of Hedgehog ligand KIF7 promotes processing of GLI3 into its transcriptional repressor form; biallelic loss-of-function KIF7 mutations deregulate most GLI target genes and impair GLI3 processing, disrupting the tightly dosed Hedgehog output required for skeletal, limb, and craniofacial patterning. The homozygous p.N1060S missense mutation identified in Al-Gazali type multiple epiphyseal dysplasia is a hypomorphic allele whose partial activity is proposed to account for the distinctive skeletal presentation.
nodes.id: KIF7 Cilium-Tip Dysfunction and Impaired Hedgehog Signal Transduction

evidence.reference_title: A mutation in KIF7 is responsible for the autosomal recessive syndrome of macrocephaly, multiple epiphyseal dysplasia and distinctive facial appearance.
evidence.snippet: the identification of a homozygous p.N1060S missense mutation in a highly conserved residue in KIF7, a regulator of Hedgehog signaling

evidence.reference_title: KIF7 mutations cause fetal hydrolethalus and acrocallosal syndromes.
evidence.snippet: KIF7, the human ortholog of Drosophila Costal2, is a key component of the Hedgehog signaling pathway

evidence.reference_title: KIF7 mutations cause fetal hydrolethalus and acrocallosal syndromes.
evidence.snippet: we show deregulation of most GLI transcription factor targets and impaired GLI3 processing in tissues from individuals with KIF7 mutations

evidence.reference_title: KIF7 mutations cause fetal hydrolethalus and acrocallosal syndromes.
evidence.snippet: Our data show the role of KIF7 in human primary cilia, especially in the Hedgehog pathway through the regulation of GLI targets, and expand the clinical spectrum of ciliopathies

nodes.description: Macrocephaly (enlarged head) is a consistent feature of the syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: A mutation in KIF7 is responsible for the autosomal recessive syndrome of macrocephaly, multiple epiphyseal dysplasia and distinctive facial appearance.
evidence.snippet: macrocephaly, multiple epiphyseal dysplasia and distinctive facial appearance

nodes.description: Multiple epiphyseal dysplasia (abnormal development of the epiphyses of multiple long bones) is the distinctive skeletal feature of this KIF7 disorder and the basis for its name.

evidence.reference_title: A mutation in KIF7 is responsible for the autosomal recessive syndrome of macrocephaly, multiple epiphyseal dysplasia and distinctive facial appearance.
evidence.snippet: macrocephaly, multiple epiphyseal dysplasia and distinctive facial appearance

nodes.description: Deregulated KIF7-dependent Hedgehog/GLI3 signaling disrupts dosed skeletal and craniofacial patterning. The hallmark consequence in Al-Gazali type is multiple epiphyseal dysplasia (abnormal development of the epiphyses of the long bones), accompanied by macrocephaly and a distinctive facial appearance. This skeletal-predominant presentation distinguishes the disorder from the brain- and limb-predominant allelic KIF7 ciliopathies.

evidence.reference_title: A mutation in KIF7 is responsible for the autosomal recessive syndrome of macrocephaly, multiple epiphyseal dysplasia and distinctive facial appearance.
evidence.snippet: The phenotype in our patients partially overlaps with the phenotypes associated with those syndromes but they also exhibit some distinctive features including multiple epiphyseal dysplasia
